data.title: Qualcomm Announces Quarterly Cash Dividend
data.description: Qualcomm Incorporated (NASDAQ: QCOM) based in San Diego, declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.92 per common share. The payment is scheduled for June 25, 2026, to shareholders on record as of June 4, 2026. Qualcomm is a technology company focused on driving innovation in intelligent computing globally. With 40 years of experience, Qualcomm aims to address significant challenges by providing a wide range of cutting-edge solutions.

data.title: Bruker Spatial Biology Showcases High Fidelity Spatial Data and Integrated Multi-platform Workflows for Unprecedented Multiomic Insights at AACR 2026
data.description: In San Diego, Bruker Corporation (BRKR) revealed the latest developments from Bruker Spatial Biology set to be presented at the 2026 American Association for Cancer Research (AACR) Annual Meeting. The event will feature the unveiling of new cross-platform workflows aimed at enhancing understanding of oncology biology and expediting translational research. Bruker Spatial Biology's innovative spatial platforms are intended to work in conjunction to provide enhanced insights. The updates will be showcased at AACR 2026, demonstrating the company's commitment to advancing cancer research.

data.title: /C O R R E C T I O N -- Concert Golf Partners/
data.description: Concert Golf Partners' acquisition of The Club at New Seabury has been ranked as the top deal of the year by Golf Inc. Magazine. The news release was issued on 17-Apr-2026 by Concert Golf Partners through PR Newswire. The purchase of The Club at New Seabury was highlighted as the number one deal in the industry. This acquisition marks a significant achievement for Concert Golf Partners, showcasing their successful transaction. The recognition from Golf Inc. Magazine further solidifies the importance and impact of this purchase in the golf industry.

data.title: Viz.ai Wins Gold Edison Award™ for Advancing AI-Driven Clinical Workflows to Improve Hemorrhage Care
data.description: In San Francisco, Viz.ai, a prominent player in AI-driven disease detection and care coordination, revealed their triumph at the 2026 Gold Edison Award for their Viz Hemorrhage solution. This accolade signifies their fourth Edison Award win and emphasizes Viz.ai's position as a pioneer in establishing the necessary framework that empowers artificial intelligence to function within healthcare institutions. The award, received in the Health, Medical & Biotech division, attests to Viz.ai's innovative approach in developing AI technologies.

data.title: U.S. Enterprises Accelerate Mainframe Modernization
data.description: Business advisory firm ISG reports that companies in the United States are updating their mainframe systems to align with current needs, placing emphasis on business operations and leveraging AI-based automation. This strategic move demonstrates a shift towards modernization and optimization for enterprises, with significant implications for enhancing efficiency and productivity.

data.title: Decisions adopted in the Ordinary General Meeting of Shareholders of AB Amber Grid
data.description: During the Ordinary General Meeting of Shareholders of AB Amber Grid on April 17, 2026, several resolutions were passed. AB Amber Grid, with legal entity code 303090867 and located at Laisvės pr. 10, LT-04215 Vilnius, Lithuania, saw key decisions made.

data.title: iPower Adds Over $2.6 Million in Contracted, Non-Dilutive Income Through Sublease Agreement
data.description: The agreement solidifies the asset-light approach and secures a steady rental income from the current infrastructure. This move is in line with the company's strategy to reduce ownership of physical assets and focus on generating revenue through rentals. The agreement is set to bring in a significant amount of recurring income, strengthening the company's financial position.

data.title: XRP Surges 12% From Its April Lows: What's Going On?
data.description: XRP has seen a significant rise of 12.5% from its low in April, coinciding with U.S. spot ETFs experiencing a noteworthy $41.64 million in net inflows for the week ending April 16. This marks the largest weekly inflow in three months and represents a 254% increase from the previous week's inflow of $11.75 million. The consistent demand over the last five days is evident through inflows of $11.87 million, $17.11 million, $11.20 million, $1.46 million, and $9.09 million. The total cumulative inflows have now reached $1.26 billion, surpassing total assets of $1.08 billion. Among the ETF products, Bitwise and Franklin are leading in terms of inflows, with Grayscale displaying mixed activity due to earlier outflows affecting the overall totals. The recent surge in numbers suggests potential pattern breakouts rather than consolidation, indicating an acceleration in market activity.

data.title: Once Upon a Farm Achieves WIC Approval in California, Reaching 20-State Milestone
data.description: As of April 17, 2026, families participating in the Women, Infants, and Children (WIC) nutrition program are now able to buy Once Upon a Farm Baby Food Bowls and specific pouches at various retailers across the state. Once Upon a Farm™, a company based in Berkeley, California, announced this new development. This expansion offers WIC-enrolled families increased access to a wider variety of nutritious food options for their young children. The specific retailers offering these products can be found statewide.
